Nxera Pharma Co., Ltd. proposes changes to its Board of Directors with nominations for External Directors. Vote scheduled for March 26.
news.summary: On February 14, 2025, Nxera Pharma Co., Ltd. in Tokyo, Japan and Cambridge, UK, disclosed a significant update. The company, listed on TSE 4565, revealed that its Board of Directors has sanctioned alterations in its directorial body. The selection of External Directors was predicated on the decision of the Nomination Committee. These appointments are anticipated to receive formal approval, pending the adoption of resolutions during the 35th Ordinary General Meeting of Shareholders. The meeting is set to convene on March 26, 2025.

news.headline: Huhtamaki to split Fiber Foodservice into Fiber Packaging and Foodservice Europe-Asia-Oceania by April 1, 2025.
news.summary: On February 14, 2025, at 08:35 EEST, Huhtamaki Oyj announced its decision to divide the Fiber Foodservice Europe-Asia-Oceania business segment into two separate segments: Fiber Packaging and Foodservice Europe-Asia-Oceania. This restructuring is scheduled to be implemented no later than April 1, 2025. The company aims to streamline operations and enhance focus within these specific areas.

news.headline: Sara Engber, 56, appointed President of Fiber Packaging at Huhtamaki, to report to CEO Ralf K. Wunderlich.
news.summary: On February 14, 2025, Huhtamäki Oyj announced Sara Engber, aged 56, as the new President of Fiber Packaging and a member of the Global Executive Team. She will be reporting directly to the President and CEO, Ralf K. Wunderlich. Engber will be stationed in Espoo, Finland, taking on this crucial role within the company.

news.headline: Vow ASA secures EUR 10.9 million order for new cruise ship with potential for two more, deliveries in late 2025 and 2026.
news.summary: A significant European shipyard has recently awarded Vow ASA (stock symbol OSE: VOW) and its subsidiary Scanship a purchase order valued at EUR 10.9 million. The deliveries are scheduled to commence in late 2025 and continue into 2026.

Aker ASA's NAV reaches NOK 58.2 billion; proposes NOK 26.50 per share cash dividend in Q4 2024.
news.summary: In Oslo, Norway, as of February 14, 2025, the Net Asset Value ("NAV") of Aker ASA and its holding companies stood at NOK 58.2 billion by the conclusion of the fourth quarter of 2024 following dividend distributions. This marked an increase from NOK 57.0 billion recorded at the close of the third quarter. Aker also distributed a dividend...

news.headline: 2024 Umicore full year financial results released, showing increase in revenue and profit.
Nxera Pharma updates operations & reports Q4 results; Full year results ended Dec 31, 2024, available.
news.summary: On February 14, 2025, Nxera Pharma Co., Ltd., based in Tokyo, Japan and Cambridge, UK, announced updates on its operational activities and released its consolidated financial results for the fourth quarter and full 12 months ending on December 31, 2024. The Company, listed as TSE 4565, shared this information in a recent report, accessible for viewing.

news.headline: Novaturas Group earns EUR 9.1 mln in January, down by 15% compared to previous year, serving 9k customers.
news.summary: Preliminary data reveals that in January, Novaturas Group recorded revenues of EUR 9.1 million, indicating a 15% decline from January 2024, where earnings were EUR 10.7 million. The company catered to 9,000 customers, compared to 9,700 during the corresponding period in the previous year.

news.headline: Diös Fastigheter's Year-end Report 2024 shows focus on transactions - divested non-core properties in first 6 months, then concentrated on growth.
news.summary: In Östersund, Sweden, on February 14, 2025, it was reported that a remarkable year of financial activity had concluded. Over the course of twelve months, a strategic approach was taken, with the initial six months dedicated to enhancing the balance sheet through the sale of non-essential assets. This phase was followed by a strong emphasis on expansion and development in the latter half of the year, resulting in substantial growth in key areas.

news.headline: Huhtamäki Oyj had a solid year with improved market conditions; financial statement release on 14.2.2025 at 8:30.
news.summary: Financial results for Huhtamäki Oyj for the period of January 1 to December 31, 2024, have been released on February 14, 2025, at 8:30 AM. The company has reported a solid performance during the year in a market that showed gradual improvement. The specific numbers detailing the financial performance of Huhtamäki Oyj for the mentioned period have been disclosed.

news.headline: KPN and ABP establish new Tower Company "Althio" after approval from Dutch Authority for Consumers & Markets on Feb 6, 2025. Operations begin today.
news.summary: Royal KPN N.V. (KPN) and ABP have completed the transaction to establish a new Tower Company, named Althio. The agreement was signed on 5 June 2024, and received approval from the Dutch Authority for Consumers & Markets on 6 February 2025. Althio (www.althio.nl) is now officially operational as of today.

news.headline: Coinbase CEO Brian Armstrong plans to add DEXes to handle meme coin surge in rising 1M weekly tokens, compares to early web.
news.summary: Coinbase Global Inc.'s CEO, Brian Armstrong, recently emphasized the need to reexamine the token listing process amidst a growing trend of meme coin launches. In the fourth-quarter 2024 earnings call, Armstrong noted the rapid evolution of the cryptocurrency sector, witnessing approximately 1 million new tokens introduced weekly. He expressed concerns about the majority of these tokens being of "lower quality" and classified as meme coins. Armstrong likened the current scenario to the early days of the Internet when all major websites could fit into a single directory, suggesting the need for improved sorting mechanisms akin to Google Search.

news.headline: China’s President Xi to chair symposium attended by Alibaba's Ma. $BABA
Bioretec Ltd's financial statements for 2024 show foundation for future growth. CE mark received in Jan 2025.
news.summary: TAMPERE, Finland  - On February 14, 2025, Bioretec Ltd made a company announcement at 8:00 a.m. detailing their financial performance for January to December 2024. The full financial statements bulletin, including tables, was released providing a comprehensive overview of the company's results during the stated period.

news.headline: Berkshire Hathaway sold $36 million of DaVita shares, now holding 35.892 million shares with 45% stake. DaVita's Q4 revenue beats estimates at $3.3 billion.
news.summary: Berkshire Hathaway Inc. (NYSE:BRK) has reduced its stake in DaVita Inc. (NYSE:DVA) by selling 203,091 shares, valued at $35.96 million based on the closing price. Berkshire now holds approximately 35.892 million shares, representing a 45% stake post share repurchase. The company has held DaVita since Q4 of 2011. DaVita reported full-year revenue of $12.8 billion and Q4 revenue of $3.3 billion, surpassing estimates. Earnings per share were $3.09 for the quarter and $10.73 for the year. This information was disclosed in a U.S. Securities Exchange Commission filing and the full story can be found on Benzinga.com.

news.headline: Court approves 60-day halt in Binance vs. SEC case, prompting Ripple and Coinbase to review legal matters amid SEC changes.
news.summary: Other major players in the cryptocurrency industry, such as Ripple and Coinbase, could be compelled to review their legal battles with the Securities and Exchange Commission (SEC) in light of the 60-day suspension. This move may signify a shift in the approach taken by key companies in the sector regarding regulatory matters.

news.headline: US Business Inventories MoM Actual 0.1% (Forecast -0.1%, Previous 0.0%)
Global electricity demand expected to rise significantly, according to IEA report.
BUYING SQUARE introduces 'Catalog Shop,' a B2B platform for brand wholesale, aiming to simplify transactions globally.
news.summary: A new digital wholesale platform called BUYING SQUARE, based in Seoul, South Korea, has revealed the international debut of Catalog Shop, a B2B tool aiming to simplify wholesale dealings for global brands. This initiative comes with the goal of enhancing the efficiency of wholesale transactions across the globe. The platform is designed to facilitate smoother processes for businesses engaged in wholesale activities and is set to revolutionize the sector. The unveiling of Catalog Shop marks a significant development for BUYING SQUARE, as it looks to offer a solution that can redefine the way wholesalers operate. This move is expected to benefit a wide range of brands and businesses on a global scale by providing a user-friendly platform to enhance their wholesale experiences.

news.headline: 500 new Starbucks stores to be opened in the Middle East over next 5 years.
Galderma's Nemluvio® approved in EU for atopic dermatitis and prurigo nodularis. Subcutaneous for age 12+ with systemic therapy.
news.summary: In Switzerland, Galderma (SWX:GALD) revealed that Nemluvio has received approval from the European Commission for treating moderate-to-severe atopic dermatitis and prurigo nodularis across the European Union (EU). Specifically, Nemluvio is sanctioned for subcutaneous administration, catering to individuals aged 12 years and above with moderate-to-severe atopic dermatitis requiring systemic therapy. Moreover, it is also cleared for subcutaneous usage in adults struggling with moderate-to-severe prurigo nodularis.

news.headline: Norsk Hydro proposes NOK 2.25 per share dividend for 2024 to General Meeting of Shareholders.
news.summary: The Norsk Hydro ASA Board of Directors has recommended a dividend of NOK 2.25 for each share to be proposed at the upcoming General Meeting. This decision is based on the company's financial results for the year 2024.

news.headline: Web3 gaming's activity surged, with 3x rise YoY in January. DappRadar revealed 7M daily active wallets, up 386% from 2024.
news.summary: According to recent data from DappRadar, the Web3 gaming sector saw a significant increase in daily unique active wallets last month, with a total of 7 million users. This marks a substantial 386% rise from the numbers reported back in January 2024.

news.headline: Norsk Hydro's Bauxite & Alumina division achieved record results with Q4 adjusted EBITDA of NOK 7.7 billion, up from NOK 3.7 billion last year.
news.summary: In the fourth quarter of 2024, Hydro reported an adjusted EBITDA of NOK 7,701 million, marking a significant increase from NOK 3,737 million in the same period of the previous year. This boost was primarily driven by higher alumina and all-in aluminium prices, as well as positive currency effects. However, these gains were somewhat mitigated by increased raw material costs, decreased Extrusions volumes and recycling margins, higher fixed and miscellaneous costs, including a tax settlement in Aluminium Metal, and a provision for upcoming social projects in Bauxite & Alumina. Hydro also achieved NOK 1.7 billion in free cash flow during Q4, with the 2024 adjusted Return on Average Capital Employed (RoaCE) closing at 8.5 percent.

Nippon Express Holdings acquires all shares in SH HoldCo GmbH, making it a wholly-owned subsidiary. Feb 14, 2025.
news.summary: NIPPON EXPRESS HOLDINGS, INC. has successfully finalized the acquisition of all shares in SH HoldCo GmbH as of February 14, 2025. The agreement for this acquisition was initially reached on September 30, 2024. This subsidiaryization marks an important milestone for NIPPON EXPRESS HOLDINGS, INC. as it expands its global presence.

Bitcoin trading points towards $125,000 aim by June; Derive predicts 44% likelihood of hitting the target, up from below $100,000.
news.summary: As of now, Bitcoin continues to stay below the $100,000 mark. Nonetheless, there is optimism surrounding its potential to reach $125,000 by the end of June. Dr. Sean Dawson of Derive mentions a 44% chance of this milestone being achieved.

news.headline: AST SpaceMobile, Inc. (NASDAQ:ASTS) gains premarket after FCC approval for testing of 5 satellites in US, enhancing cell service capabilities with AT&T and Verizon.
news.summary: AST SpaceMobile, Inc. (NASDAQ:ASTS) is seeing an increase in premarket trading on Friday following the company's receipt of Special Temporary Authority (STA) from the FCC for testing in the U.S. The approval allows AST SpaceMobile's first five commercial BlueBird satellites, currently in low Earth orbit, to utilize AT&T and Verizon's premium low-band spectrum with unmodified smartphones. These satellites can support voice, full data, video applications, and other cellular broadband capabilities without the need for specialized software or devices. The FCC's approval aligns with AST SpaceMobile's goals of expanding digital access, improving emergency communications, and hastening digital transformation.

news.headline: Top mid-cap gainers last week: Akero Therapeutics surged 106.57%, Brinker International rose 25.44%, and Viavi climbed 24.40%.
news.summary: These ten mid-cap stocks were the top performers in the previous week, prompting investors to take notice of their potential. Akero Therapeutics, Inc. (NASDAQ: AKRO) saw an impressive surge of 106.57% following the release of promising Week 96 results from its SYMMETRY Phase 2B study of Efruxifermin. Analysts responded by raising their price forecasts for the stock. Surgery Partners, Inc. (NASDAQ: SGRY) experienced a 25.44% increase in its stock value after revealing a non-binding acquisition proposition from Bain Capital Private Equity. Similarly, Flagstar Financial, Inc. (NYSE: FLG) witnessed a significant rise of 24.40% thanks to better-than-expected financial results. Analyst Jared Shaw from Barclays maintained an Overweight rating on the stock. For more details, the full story is available on Benzinga.com. Are these stocks part of your investment portfolio?

news.headline: Fabrinet beats Q2 revenue at $833.6 million with adjusted EPS of $2.61. Datacom demand moderates. CEO cites strong telecom performance.
news.summary: Fabrinet's (NYSE:FN) premarket trading on Tuesday saw shares dip following the release of second-quarter results. The company reported revenues of $833.6 million, surpassing the expected $814.3 million consensus. Additionally, its adjusted EPS stood at $2.61, higher than the anticipated $2.50. Seamus Grady, CEO of Fabrinet, highlighted the robust performance in the telecom sector, citing the growing demand for datacenter interconnect products and positive outcomes from recent system victories. Despite a slight downturn in datacom demand in the short run... (Note: The full story can be found on the Benzinga website.)

news.headline: Coffee prices have surged over 100%, marking highest levels since 1977. Arabica coffee futures hit $3.90 per pound.
news.summary: The price of coffee is surging to record highs, with Arabica coffee futures climbing for 10 consecutive sessions, reaching $3.90 per pound which is a 100% increase from a year ago. This surge in coffee prices, the best-performing asset of 2025 so far, raises concerns about potential skyrocketing inflation on daily coffee cups. With 66% of Americans consuming 2-3 cups daily, the U.S. being the largest coffee market, the impact is significant. The possibility of a $10 cup of coffee looms, posing challenges for consumers and the supply chain and also creating potential opportunities for companies in the coffee industry.

news.headline: Huntington Ingalls misses Q4 earnings, with sales at $3.00B, down from $3.05B. Operating income drops 64.7% Y/Y. EPS down 54.3% Y/Y.
news.summary: Shares of Huntington Ingalls Industries Inc (NYSE:HII) dropped following the release of its fourth-quarter FY24 results. Sales and service revenue saw a 5.4% year-over-year increase, reaching $3.00 billion, falling short of the expected $3.05 billion consensus. Ingalls Shipbuilding experienced a revenue of $736 million, down by 8.0% Y/Y, while Newport News Shipbuilding reached $1.59 billion (-4.6% Y/Y), and Mission Technologies reported $713 million (-4.3% Y/Y). Operating income took a hit, decreasing by 64.7% Y/Y to $110 million, causing the margin to contract by 616 bps to 3.7%. Segment operating income was $103 million (-68.8% Y/Y), with a margin of 3.4%, decreasing by 696 bps Y/Y. The EPS landed at $3.15, down 54.3% Y/Y from the consensus of $3.31. Operating cash flow for 2024 plummeted to $393 million, while free cash flow dropped to $40 million, in comparison to $970 million and $692 million respectively.

news.headline: Walgreens transfers 6.1M Cencora shares and pays $20M in early settlement, sells 1.3M shares for $300M. Retains 12.6M shares under remaining contracts.
news.summary: Walgreens Boots Alliance, Inc. (NASDAQ: WBA) recently announced an agreement for the early settlement of prepaid variable share forward transactions connected to Cencora, Inc. (NYSE: COR). In this settlement, Walgreens Boots will transfer roughly 6.1 million Cencora shares to financial institutions and will also provide a net cash payment of around $20 million. Additionally, the company completed the sale of the remaining 1.3 million Cencora shares from the early settled contracts, generating approximately $300 million. After this transaction, WBA still holds 12.6 million Cencora shares under existing prepaid contracts. The full story is accessible on Benzinga.com.

Netherlands December imports decline 1.1% year on year
XRP gains 4% as SEC acknowledges Grayscale ETF filing, outshining BTC and ETH which fell 0.33% and 1.18%.
news.summary: XRP experienced a significant surge in value on Thursday after the SEC recognized an ETF application by Grayscale Investments. The third-largest cryptocurrency saw a gain of over 4% in the last 24 hours, contrasting with a slight decrease of 0.33% for Bitcoin and 1.18% for Ethereum during the same period. The SEC's call for comments on Grayscale's ETF application contributed to XRP's outperformance. This move follows a public request for submissions within 21 days of publication.

news.headline: Discover Guangzhou's booming industry along the Pearl River, producing cheap goods exported globally. #China #exports #Guangzhou
news.summary: In southern China along the Pearl River, there is a rapidly expanding industry that is producing inexpensive clothing and toys. These products are being exported worldwide without any duty fees.
